user.title: Connect PPC Smart Meter Gateway (by Lutarym)
user.description:
Enter the IP address or hostname of your gateway. It will be checked for reachability on port 443 before you can enter login credentials.

Installed integration version: {version}

credentials.title: Gateway login

meters.title: Select meters

tariffs.title: Select evaluation profiles

history.title: Optional: import historical data
history.description:
Current live value of 1-0:1.8.0 (grid draw): **{current_value}** - this is used automatically as the end point, no need to enter it.

All fields below are optional. Leave them empty to skip this step entirely. To backfill history: enter a start date, the known correct value at that date, and a source entity whose shape (day-to-day variation) should be used for interpolation between the start date and now. Where the source entity itself has no data (e.g. before it was installed), that portion is interpolated evenly over time.

If you already know the exact consumption (kWh) for specific months, enter it below - it takes priority over the source entity's estimate for that month. Leave a month empty to derive it automatically.

Installed integration version: {version}

error.invalid_auth: Login rejected by the gateway (HTTP 401) - username or password is incorrect.
error.parsing_error: Login was successful (credentials are correct!), but the gateway's response page could not be read. See details below.
error.cannot_connect: Could not reach the gateway on port 443. Check the IP address and network route.
error.no_meters_found: No meters were found on the gateway. See details below.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the gateway.

import_history.name: Import history
import_history.description: One-time import of a corrected historical statistics series for the 1-0:1.8.0 (grid draw) sensor - combines a start anchor, the current live value as end anchor, a source entity's day-to-day shape, and optional known monthly values.

start_date.name: Start date
start_date.description: The date the historical series should start at.

start_value.name: Start value (kWh)
start_value.description: The known correct cumulative value at the start date, in kWh.

source_entity.name: Source entity
source_entity.description: Entity whose statistics provide the day-to-day shape for interpolation (e.g. a less accurate but gapless alternative meter). Where this entity itself has no data (e.g. before it was installed), that portion is interpolated evenly over time.

target_entity.name: Target entity (optional)
target_entity.description: The 1-0:1.8.0 sensor to write to. Auto-detected if left empty - only works automatically when exactly one gateway is configured.

monthly_kwh.name: Known monthly values (optional)
monthly_kwh.description: Map of "YYYY-MM": kWh for months where the exact consumption is already known - takes priority over the source entity's estimate for that month. Example: {"2026-01": 130.5, "2026-03": 95}

dry_run.name: Dry run
dry_run.description: If enabled, only computes and returns a summary (incl. monthly breakdown) without writing anything to the statistics.
